title: The Spectacular
author: Fiona Davis
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Spectacular" book by Fiona Davis?
Options:
A. Hornsby. He said to be ready when the car comes at eleven. Let me know if you need help with your hair. Thank you, Mrs. Hornsby. I know this is as hard for you as it is for the rest of us. After drinking a cup of coffee, Marion changed into a black dress. The pearls were back in her jewelry box, and she clasped them around her neck before dissolving into tears. It was probably better that Lucille wasnt around to have to bear the pain of this day and all the days to come. Would her mother also have considered Marion complicit in the death of her sister? Even though her father hadnt mentioned that terrible accusation hed made that night at the hospital, Marion couldnt help but sense that it simmered just below the surface of their every interaction.
B. Hornsby instructed her to be ready when the car comes at 11 o'clock. He inquired whether she requires help with her hair. Marion expressed gratitude to Mrs. Hornsby. She understands this is as hard for Hornsby as it is for the rest of them. Marion changed into a black dress after drinking a cup of coffee. She clasped the pearls from her jewelry box around her neck before bursting into tears. It was most likely preferable that Lucille was not around to bear the anguish of this day and all the upcoming days. Would her mother also consider Marion complicit in Lucille's passing? Even if her father had not brought up his awful accusation from the hospital, Marion could not help but feel it simmered just below every interaction they had.
C. Hornsby told Marion to be prepared when the car arrives at 11 AM. He asked if she needs help styling her hair. Marion thanked Mrs. Hornsby, knowing this is as difficult for her as everyone else. After drinking coffee, Marion changed into a black dress. She took her pearl necklace from her jewelry box and put it on before crying. It was probably for the best that Lucille was not here to experience the pain of this day and future days. Would her mother also think Marion was involved in Lucille's death? Even though her father did not repeat his terrible hospital accusation, Marion sensed it lurking beneath their every interaction.
D. Hornsby told her to be prepared when the car arrives at 11 AM. He asked if she needs assistance with her hair. Marion thanked Mrs. Hornsby. She knows this is as difficult for Hornsby as it is for everyone. After drinking coffee, Marion put on a black dress. She took the pearls from her jewelry box and put them on before crying. It was probably better that Lucille was not present to experience the agony of this day and future days. Would her mother also think Marion was involved in her sister's death? Even though her father did not mention his terrible hospital accusation, Marion sensed it was underlying their every interaction.
Answer:

Even though her father hadnt mentioned that terrible accusation hed made that night at the hospital, Marion couldnt help but sense that it simmered just below the surface of their every interaction.
